name: Loan For Any Purpose
description_short: Detroit-based multi-product lender offering cash advances, payday loans, title loans, and personal loans from $250 to $50,000. Most products accept all credit types.
description_long:
Loan For Any Purpose is a consumer lending company operating physical branch locations in Detroit, Michigan and Atlanta, Georgia. The Detroit branch is located at 3403 Grand River Ave #200 (48208) and maintains extended seven-day hours — Monday through Friday 7am to 8pm and Saturday through Sunday 9am to 6pm — positioning itself as an accessible lender for borrowers across the credit spectrum under the tagline "Need Quick Cash? No Problem!"

The company's product lineup spans the full range of short-term and medium-term consumer credit. At the small-dollar end, they offer cash advances and payday loans for amounts under $1,000, as well as same-day loans requiring minimal documentation. For mid-range needs, unsecured installment loans are available from $1,000 to $5,000. For larger borrowing, both personal loans and title loans reach up to $50,000. Title loans are secured by the borrower's vehicle and open to all credit types; personal loans are unsecured but explicitly note a higher credit score is recommended. The overall stated borrowing range is $250 to $50,000.

One notable feature is that Loan For Any Purpose does not perform hard FICO inquiries during the application process. Instead, they may pull from non-traditional consumer reporting agencies, meaning applying does not damage a borrower's FICO score. The company also complies with USA PATRIOT Act account-opening requirements. Their disclosures are relatively transparent: they acknowledge their products are designed for short-term needs and explicitly recommend certified credit counseling for borrowers with ongoing financial difficulties — an unusual disclosure that signals some awareness of debt-cycle risk.

Loan For Any Purpose is a practical option for Detroit-area borrowers with bad or thin credit who need fast access to funds and have few alternatives. However, the Detroit location page does not publish APRs, interest rates, or fee schedules, which makes it impossible to evaluate the true cost of borrowing before applying. Their own disclaimer warns that these products "are not intended as a long-term borrowing solution," which is worth taking seriously — particularly for payday loans and title loans, which typically carry very high APRs and, in the case of title loans, put the borrower's vehicle at risk.
pros: Applying does not affect your FICO score — company uses non-traditional reporting agencies instead of hard pulls
Seven-day availability with extended weekday hours (Mon-Fri 7am-8pm, Sat-Sun 9am-6pm)
All credit types accepted for most products — cash advances, payday loans, title loans, installment loans, and bad credit loans
Same-day loan option available with minimum documents required
Broad loan range ($250–$50,000) covering everything from emergency cash to larger vehicle-secured loans
Both secured (title loans) and unsecured (personal, installment, cash advance) loan types available
Physical branch with direct local phone number — not purely online
cons: No APR, interest rates, or fee schedules published on the location page
Personal loans (largest unsecured option) require a higher credit score, limiting access for bad-credit borrowers
Online applications may take up to two additional business days for verification before funds are disbursed
Company's own disclosures state products are not suitable for ongoing or long-term borrowing needs
Title loans put the borrower's vehicle at risk as collateral if repayment fails
best_for: Detroit-area borrowers needing same-day cash under $1,000 with bad or no credit history
Vehicle owners seeking title loans up to $50,000 without a strict credit score requirement
Borrowers needing installment loans ($1,000–$5,000) who cannot qualify at traditional lenders
Anyone who wants to check loan options without triggering a hard FICO inquiry
diagnosis: Best for Detroit-area borrowers with bad or thin credit who need fast access to cash between $250 and $50,000, including those with a vehicle to use as collateral for larger title loans. The primary caveat is that the company does not disclose rates or APRs publicly, and their own disclosures explicitly warn these are short-term products not suited for ongoing borrowing — the debt-cycle risk is real, particularly for repeat payday or cash advance users.
services: Title loans (up to $50,000, secured by vehicle title)
Personal loans (up to $50,000, higher credit score required)
In-person branch lending at Detroit MI location
